description: Offline health guidelines and quick reference cards

sections.heading: Choking (Heimlich Manoeuvre)
steps: Give 5 firm back blows between shoulder blades

sections.heading: Burns
steps: Cool with running water for 10-20 minutes
Do NOT apply ice, butter, toothpaste or oil
Seek hospital care for large or deep burns

sections.heading: Snake Bite
steps: Immobilise the bitten limb (keep still and low)
Do NOT cut, suck, or apply tourniquet
Rush to nearest hospital with anti-venom IMMEDIATELY

sections.heading: CPR (Adult)
steps: Place heel of hand on centre of chest
Press hard and fast — 30 compressions at 100-120/min

sections.heading: WHO-Recommended ORS Recipe
steps: Start with 1 litre of clean/boiled water
Add ½ level teaspoon of salt
Give small sips frequently — do not force large amounts
Discard and make fresh after 24 hours

sections.heading: When to Use ORS
steps: Diarrhea — start ORS at first loose stool
Vomiting — give small sips every 5 minutes
Seek hospital if unable to keep ORS down or blood in stool

sections.heading: Children (1–12 years)
steps: Blood pressure: lower than adults (normal range varies by age)

sections.heading: What is TB?
steps: Tuberculosis (TB) is an infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis
Spreads through the air when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or talks
Curable with proper treatment — do NOT stop early

sections.heading: DOTS Treatment
steps: Take ALL medications every day — never skip
Treatment is FREE at government health centres
